delivered.fact: While you sleep, your brain doesn't just rest: during deep NREM sleep, three distinct brain rhythms synchronise perfectly — slow oscillations, sleep spindles, and sharp-wave ripples — to physically transfer fragile new memories from the hippocampus to permanent long-term storage in the cortex. This process also clears encoding capacity so your brain is ready to learn new things the following day. This mechanism has been consistently replicated across independent studies.

Desert ants are the first insect confirmed to use the *polarity* of Earth's magnetic field for navigation, not just its inclination. Unlike every other known animal magnetic sense, they can tell north from south directly. This was verified in 2024 through controlled field experiments where researchers flipped local magnetic fields, and the ants turned exactly 180 degrees accordingly.
